steps.title: Identify the grammatical relationships
steps.content: The main clause already has a finite verb later in the sentence (“adapted the test”). The blank therefore should not introduce a second finite verb — it should begin a participial phrase that modifies scientists.

steps.title: Apply the correct verb form
steps.content: Choice B (searching) is the present participle that correctly forms the participial phrase “searching for evidence of self-awareness in snakes…” modifying scientists, with “adapted” serving as the main verb. Choice A (“searched”) makes two finite verbs without a connector. Choice C (“were searching”) and D (“have searched”) likewise create a duplicate finite verb. That matches choice B — B is correct.
